explanatory_strength.rationale: Four additive components tie network OCF (~$4.70/sh), AAdvantage co-brand option, net debt (~$29B), and cycle reserve to a coherent capital-cycle story with explicit overlap controls. The competitive-advantage mechanism is weak: moat is none, fares are commoditized, and the mispricing thesis rests on complexity discount and deleveraging optionality rather than a durable toll or widening barrier.

evidence_sufficiency.rationale: FY2025 10-K and Q1 2026 10-Q anchor revenue ($54.6B), operating income ($1.5B, down from $2.6B), OCF ($3.1B), capex ($3.8B), and debt ($29B) with 100% calculation-proof coverage. Gaps remain for Hohn fieldwork: management_facts is empty, AAdvantage co-brand base ($8/sh) is a judgment band without standalone disclosure or success probability, and Lawrence synthesis (28.48%) diverges from filing-locked component sum (−$9.79/sh base).

downside_control.rationale: Dhando is none; bounded-bear gate fails at the equity layer. Net financial claims base −$42.67/sh and cycle reserve −$9.00/sh quantify leverage and cyclical stress, but component low case (−$41.70/sh) implies severe downside versus $15.14 spot. FY2025 capex exceeded OCF and cash is only $902M against ~$29B debt, leaving little cushion if earnings trough coincides with refinancing.

return_vs_alternatives.rationale: Contract base annualized return at price is null; high case is only 8.12% versus a ~15% hurdle. Lawrence/synthesis paths show 27–28% base mechanically, but proof-first component economics are negative at base (−$9.79/sh). Hohn return screen fails on filing-locked proof; the levered stub requires trusting mid-cycle normalization and loyalty monetization beyond what competitive-advantage evidence supports.

claims.claim: FY2025 operating cash flow was $3.1B (~$4.70/sh) on $54.6B revenue, but operating income fell to $1.5B from $2.6B prior year and capital spending ($3.8B) exceeded OCF.
Proof-first additive components sum to −$9.79/sh base ($33.88 network + $8.00 co-brand − $42.67 net debt − $9.00 cycle reserve) versus $15.14 spot, with contract base annualized return withheld.
Classification is capital_cycle with moat none and dhando none; total debt and lease obligations ~$29B against $902M cash cap equity downside before any competitive-advantage premium.

strongest_counter_explanation: The market correctly prices American as a levered cyclical equity stub, not a quality franchise: mid-cycle OCF exists but ~$28B net debt absorbs most economic value before common equity, FY2025 margin compression shows weak pricing power, and any AAdvantage loyalty toll is commingled in consolidated revenue without filing proof of separable, incremental owner cash.
most_important_missing_fact: Standalone AAdvantage co-brand and partner mile-sale economics (annual cash from card partners, margin, contract duration and renewal terms) from SEC disclosure or management, to test whether loyalty is a non-overlapping toll or already captured in network OCF.
falsifiers: Trailing four-quarter operating cash flow per share falls below $3.50 for four quarters without proportional debt reduction
Total debt rises above $32B without proportional operating income recovery for two consecutive quarters
Two consecutive quarters of operating margin below 2% while industry load factors remain above 80%, indicating structural fare weakness rather than transient fuel or labor noise
specialist_findings: Competitive-advantage audit: AAL sits outside Hohn power zone (capital_cycle archetype, moat none; Hohn seeks compounder/croupier/platform with stable or widening moats). Overlap controls are clean—network OCF, co-brand option, net financial claims, and cycle reserve use distinct overlap keys with no double_counting_flags. Valuation-proof rows reconcile arithmetically (100% proof_complete_pct). Audit concerns: (1) owner-cash capitalization multiple (4.5–10.5×) and co-brand milestone ($0–$20/sh) are bounded judgments without standalone filing anchors; (2) Lawrence base IRR (27–28%) and component sum (−$9.79/sh) tell opposite stories—the former treats equity as a cheap option on mid-cycle cash, the latter as underwater at base; (3) actual GAAP free cash flow is negative (capex > OCF), triggering Hohn falsifier on commoditized/levered profile despite positive OCF proxy; (4) zero management fieldwork this period. No approved third-party IRR corroboration.
